summary: Large item decomp matching pass that pushed item translation units over the PR's >90% target, promoted six item files from NonMatching to Matching, and replaced many placeholder/UNK layouts with typed item, collision, hurtbone, and state-table structures. The decomp-dev report recorded +12020 matched code bytes, +20644 linked code bytes, +1232 matched data bytes, 43 new matches, and 30 improvements, with only a small it_2725 .data regression noted.

key_files.role: Promoted itsamuschargeshot.c, itgamewatchchef.c, itkamex.c, itunknown.c, ithouou.c, and itwhitebea.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Adjusted data/sdata/sdata2 symbols, mostly turning compiler-local constants and strings into local @ labels and fixing one data object size/label.
Largest core item TU cleanup: item command parsing, MObj/TEV typed access, ItCo.dat/usd loading, Pokemon selection/spawning, helper ordering, effects, and scale helpers.
Major item collision matching pass using typed damage logs, hurtbone data, per-fighter tracking, helper inlines/outlines, and a typed hit-element effect table.
Introduced or refined Item_FtTrack, DamageLogEntry, ItHurtBoneDesc/List, ItemModStruct, GXColor item color fields, and ItemCommonData.xE8 as f32.
Refined item-specific attrs/vars for Kamex, Houou, WhiteBea, Oldottosea, and Unknown, including unions for float/int overlay fields.
Typed Link boomerang trail buffers and animation/material/shape animation pointers in boomerang attributes.
Added state table, matched boomerang spawn/return/trail logic, replaced M2C field hacks with xDF0/xEB0 Vec3 buffers, and changed it_802A0534 to accept an angle.
Added Samus charge shot state table and matched charge-state animation logic, including Samus/Kirby owner checks and scale calculation.
Added state table and matched itGamewatchchef_UnkMotion0_Anim; reused motion0 collision behavior for motion1 collision.
Added Houou state tables, typed Houou item vars, and matched sacred-fire spawn/path logic such as it_802D2D2C.
Added Kamex state tables, typed float attributes, matched Hydro Pump spawn logic it_802CAB10, and inlined constants for scale growth.
Added Super Scope state table and matched charge-level/cost selection helpers including it_80291DAC.
Added Unknown state table, typed randomization/velocity fields with unions, and matched spawn/rotation behavior.
Added WhiteBea state table and matched movement/collision/partner-distance logic including it_802E35CC and itWhitebea_UnkMotion2_Anim.
Changed ftCo_80098634 to take Item_FtTrack and write x150_count.
Preserved calcAnglePos result and passed it into it_802A0534 for Link boomerang.

what_changed: configure.py changed six item objects from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...): itsamuschargeshot.c, itgamewatchchef.c, itkamex.c, itunknown.c, ithouou.c, and itwhitebea.c.

The decomp-dev GALE01 report recorded 43 new matches and 30 improvements in unmatched items, including full matches for itSamuschargeshot_UnkMotion0_Anim, it_80279E24, it_802CAB10, it_802E35CC, itWhitebea_UnkMotion2_Anim, it_802D2D2C, itGamewatchchef_UnkMotion0_Anim, it_8026FE68, and multiple item .data sections.

src/melee/it/it_2725.c was heavily rewritten from M2C/placeholder-style code toward typed C: it_MObjInfo wraps HSD_ClassInfo plus MObj callbacks/templates, ItCo.dat/usd archive loading uses named strings and an it_804D6D20_t layout, item command readers parse cmd->u with explicit u8/u16/s16/u32 casts, and helper definitions such as it_80272CC0, it_80272D40, it_80272DE4, and it_80272F7C were repositioned.

src/melee/it/itcoll.c replaced generic allocation structs with DamageLogEntry and Item_FtTrack, added an itColl_chkECBOverlap inline, typed item hurtbone data via ItHurtBoneList/ItHurtBoneDesc, and cleaned up item-vs-item/fighter collision handling while keeping match-control wrappers.

Multiple item files gained real ItemStateTable arrays at their address-based global names, replacing previously absent data and contributing to .data matches for Samus charge shot, Game & Watch Chef, Kamex, Link boomerang, Super Scope, Unknown, Houou, and WhiteBea.

Item-specific structs were improved: Link boomerang got Vec3 xDF0/xEB0 trail buffers and animation/matanim/shapeanim pointers; Houou got itHouou_ItemVars; Unknown got float/int unions; WhiteBea got an Item_GObj* x20 and structured attribute pointer; Kamex attrs x8/xC/x10/x14 became floats.
Item color/modifier storage was clarified in types.h by changing xBC4..xBC7 bytes into a GXColor xBC4 and moving ItemModStruct xBC8; item.c initialization now writes xBC4.a/b/g/r fields.

Fighter/item integration was updated: ftCo_80098634 now uses Item_FtTrack; Item_804A0CCC is typed as Item_FtTrack; ftLink SpecialS now passes the calculated boomerang angle; ftcoll.h exposes ftColl_80076808 as a non-static declaration.
The symbol map changed many anonymous strings/floats/doubles from global it_804D*/it_803F* names to local @ labels and adjusted it_803F7D60 from size 0x30 to 0x40 with a local ...data.0 label.
smart_moves: Typed semantic structs were introduced only where the diff had enough evidence from use sites; unclear fields largely stayed offset-style xNN names, which improved matching without over-naming.

The Item_FtTrack rename is reusable because the diff documents its producer and consumer: ftCo_80098634 populates per-fighter ECB/position entries and it_80271B60 reads them for item/fighter ECB overlap.
The itColl_chkECBOverlap helper extracted an identical overlap test used by multiple collision routines, reducing duplicated fragile comparison code while preserving the same geometry formula.
it_8026FAC4_inline, it_8026FAC4_outline, it_8026F9AC_outline, and similar inline/outline wrappers kept call shape and inlining controllable while allowing the source to become readable.

The MObj/TEV rewrite in it_2725.c replaced raw pointer arithmetic into it_803F1F90 with a typed it_MObjInfo template, turning magic offsets into named fields like texp_tmpl, tevdesc_tmpl, assert_file, and report_fmt_no_register.
Link boomerang matching used real trail-buffer arrays and animation pointers instead of M2C_FIELD indexing, then threaded the actual calcAnglePos return value from ftLk_SpecialS into it_802A0534.
Command parsing functions in it_2725.c leaned on packed raw reads from cmd->u, explicit bit masks, and the original fixed-point scale 0.003906f, a good pattern for matching item script commands.
The symbols.txt cleanup recognized compiler-local constants and strings as local @ symbols, avoiding unnecessary global names that can hide data-section matching issues.
decomp_lessons: For item TUs, matching often depends as much on correct data layout as on function bodies; this PR matched many .data sections by adding ItemStateTable globals and correcting symbol scope.
Generic HSD_ObjAllocUnk* names should be retired when producer/consumer behavior identifies a real structure; Item_FtTrack and DamageLogEntry are examples with comments that explain their roles.
Overlay storage should be represented with unions when the same offset is used as float in one path and int in another, as done for itUnknown_ItemVars and itUnknownAttributes.
If an item command stream is packed, preserve the packed access pattern with explicit u8/u16/s16/u32 casts and bit masks rather than forcing a speculative high-level struct too early.
Small helper placement, inlining pragmas, PAD_STACK, UNUSED pad bytes, and outline wrappers are legitimate matching levers when they are localized and documented by the diff.
Header/API fixes can be necessary for item matching even outside src/melee/it: fighter callbacks and Link accessory code had to expose the right item-facing types and arguments.
Prefer typed archive/data aggregate structs over comments about '8 bytes instead of 4'; the it_804D6D20_t layout made ItCo public data loads and global assignments explicit.

When changing symbol scope, keep evidence from the object map: local compiler constants should become @ labels, but true data tables such as ItemStateTable globals should remain address-named until semantic names are known.
naming_conventions: Item motion callbacks follow it<Item>_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll, and state arrays remain address-named globals such as it_803F7288, it_803F7B08, it_803F7D60, and it_803F8A88 when no semantic table name is established.
Item-specific variable and attribute structs use item-prefixed names like itHouou_ItemVars, itWhiteBea_ItemVars, itKamexAttributes, itUnknownAttributes, and itLinkBoomerangAttributes.
Partially understood fields keep offset-style xNN names, but add type/meaning hints when evidence is strong, e.g. x150_count, x4C_anim, x50_matanim, x54_shapeanim, xC0_pos_arr, and x0_ecb_arr.
Cross-system helper structs use concise semantic names with system context, such as Item_FtTrack, DamageLogEntry, ItHurtBoneDesc, and ItHurtBoneList.

Address-based function names were retained for unknown behavior, while matched behavior-specific functions such as itGamewatchchef_UnkMotion0_Anim and itWhitebea_UnkMotion2_Anim use existing item motion naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline around small command handlers and item helpers to control codegen.
Kept PAD_STACK and UNUSED local padding where stack layout mattered.
Introduced inline and outline helper pairs to steer whether repeated logic emitted inline or as calls.
Used ABS, M_TAU, DEG2RAD, 0.003906f fixed-point scaling, and direct numeric constants where they matched original codegen and data references.
Converted some extern sdata2 constants to literal double/float expressions, e.g. Kamex scale growth using 1.0 and 0.06 instead of it_804DD358/it_804DD360.
Replaced pointer arithmetic/M2C_FIELD with typed arrays and structs after validating offset-compatible layouts.
Adjusted function signatures and call sites to remove unnecessary HSD_GObj casts and use Item_GObj/Fighter_GObj directly.
Reordered helper definitions within it_2725.c while preserving calls, likely to better match TU code ordering and local inlining behavior.
Scoped constants and strings in symbols.txt as local @ labels to better reflect compiler-emitted local data.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews are present in this slice; the PR body is empty, so reviewer intent is not evidenced.

The only feedback-like record is the decomp-dev bot report for GALE01, which reported matched code 64.94% (+0.31%, +12020 bytes), linked code 37.07% (+0.53%, +20644 bytes), matched data 38.55% (+0.10%, +1232 bytes), and linked data 32.85% (+0.08%, +1008 bytes).
The bot report also flagged one small regression: main/melee/it/it_2725 .data changed by -3 bytes, 93.58% to 93.56%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that build on Item_FtTrack or rename remaining fields in Item_FtTrack.
Search for follow-up fixes to the small it_2725 .data regression reported by decomp-dev.
Search later item PRs for remaining NonMatching item files after this promotion, especially itsamusmissile, itsamusgrapple, and itclinkmilk.
Find PRs that further type item command streams around it_802790C0, it_8027978C, and related it_2725 command handlers.
Search for additional cleanup of Link boomerang trail buffers xDF0/xEB0 and boomerang angle handling.
Find follow-up struct naming for itUnknown_ItemVars unions and itWhiteBeaAttributes x0 entry layout.
